title: Boat Rental in Halifax: Costs and Choices for 2026
html:
Exploring the waters of Halifax by boat is an unforgettable experience, with half-day rentals starting from around CAD 250 for a small motorboat. Whether you want a self-drive runabout or a crewed yacht, you can book a boat online through BoatHire24, which connects renters with local owners across the region.

How much does it cost to rent a boat in Halifax?

Prices for boat rentals in Halifax generally range from CAD 250 for a half-day motorboat to CAD 1,500 for a crewed yacht. The final cost depends on the vessel type, season, and trip duration. For a full-day catamaran charter, expect to pay between CAD 800 and CAD 1,200, while guided fishing tours start at CAD 150 per person.

  • Shared tour (per person): from CAD 100 for a two-hour harbour cruise.
  • Small motorboat (half-day): from CAD 250 for a 16-18 foot runabout.
  • Catamaran (full-day): from CAD 900 for a Lagoon 380, accommodating up to 12 guests.
  • Crewed yacht (full-day): from CAD 1,400 for a skippered Fairline 40 including fuel.

Where to set off in Halifax

Halifax boasts several well-served harbours and marinas. The main departure point is the Halifax Waterfront, with berths at Cable Wharf and the Halifax Marina. To the south, the Royal Nova Scotia Yacht Squadron in Northwest Arm offers protected moorings. For longer trips, head to Eastern Passage to access McNabs Island and Lawlor Island. The nearby town of Dartmouth, across the harbour, provides additional launch ramps and parking.

Which boat to rent

BoatHire24 offers a range of vessels suited to different needs. Choose based on your group size and preferred activity.

  1. Bowrider runabout: Best for day trips to McNabs Island (7 nautical miles from Halifax waterfront). Up to 6 guests, from CAD 250 per half-day.
  2. Centre console: Ideal for fishing in the open Atlantic around Sambro Island. Up to 8 guests, from CAD 350 per half-day.
  3. Motor yacht (Fairline 40): For comfortable family cruising to St. Margarets Bay. Up to 10 guests, from CAD 800 per day.
  4. Catamaran (Lagoon 380): Best for sunset charters with ample deck space. Up to 12 guests, from CAD 900 per day.
  5. Sailboat: Perfect for a quiet sail around the Northwest Arm. Up to 6 guests, from CAD 400 per half-day.

Among these options, the Fairline 40 is a popular choice; you can view the Fairline for hire on BoatHire24 for full specs and availability.

Do you need a licence to rent a boat in Halifax?

In Canada, a Pleasure Craft Operator Card (PCOC) is legally required to drive a powered boat. However, some rental operators may include a skipper or offer a short briefing that waives the card for first-time renters. Always check with the owner, who will confirm exact requirements when you book through BoatHire24.

In summer, the prevailing south-westerly wind can pick up in the afternoon, so plan your trip to McNabs Island for the morning to enjoy calmer seas and a sheltered return.

When is the best season for boating in Halifax?

The main boating season runs from May to September, with peak months July and August offering water temperatures above 15°C. Spring and autumn can bring fog and cooler winds, but late September often has settled conditions. In 2026, holiday weekends such as Canada Day (1 July) and Natal Day (early August) see high demand, so book early.

At a glance

  • Price range: CAD 250 (half-day motorboat) to CAD 1,500 (full-day crewed yacht).
  • Peak months: July and August.
  • Trip length: 2 hours to full day.
  • Guest capacity: 2 to 12 guests.
  • Currency: Canadian dollars (CAD).

Questions about boat rental in Halifax

Can I rent a boat without a licence in Halifax?

Some operators allow unlicensed renters if a skipper is provided, but for self-drive you generally need a PCOC.

Are there boats available for large groups?

Yes, catamarans and motor yachts accommodating up to 12 guests are commonly listed on BoatHire24.

What is included in the rental price?

Most rentals include basic safety gear, fuel for half-day trips, and sometimes a cooler; check the listing for details.

How far can I travel in a half-day?

A half-day allows a return trip to McNabs Island, about 7 nautical miles from downtown Halifax.

Whether you are a visitor or a local, renting a boat in Halifax opens up the stunning coastline and islands of Nova Scotia. To see current availability, check availability on BoatHire24. If you own a boat, consider listing it on the platform to reach thousands of renters worldwide.

BoatHire24 is a worldwide marketplace where you can rent a boat in Halifax or list your own.

Written by the BoatHire24 team, who book and list verified boats in Halifax and hundreds of destinations worldwide.
